steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The $y$-intercept is the value of $y$ when $x = 0$. Substitute: $y = 400(3)^{2(0)} = 400(1) = 400$. Since $x$ counts six-month periods starting from the end of January 1997, $x = 0$ is that starting moment. The $y$-intercept means 400 subscribers at the end of January 1997.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — first six-month period: that's $x = 1$, not $x = 0$. ✗
  • Choice B — 400 subscribers at the end of January 1997: matches the $y$-intercept exactly. ✓
  • Choice C — rate of change: the $y$-intercept is a starting value, not a rate. ✗
  • Choice D — number of six-month periods: that's what $x$ represents, not $y$. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
